expectations.text: A report.md file was created in the outputs directory
expectations.evidence: report.md exists at the expected path

expectations.text: Report correctly identifies this as a dotfiles/stow-based repo
expectations.evidence: Opening line: 'This is a well-structured GNU Stow-based dotfiles repository'

expectations.text: Report contains findings from multiple distinct analysis dimensions
expectations.evidence: Findings cover safety (stow --adopt), infrastructure (launchd PATH), shell scripting (error handling, variables), architecture (wt bash vs Go), config management (kitty/ghostty), conventions (PATH duplication)

expectations.text: Report includes a 'what's working well' section
expectations.evidence: No dedicated section for what's working well. The executive summary says 'The architecture is sound for its purpose' but no structured positives.

expectations.text: Findings reference specific file paths as evidence
expectations.evidence: All findings include file paths and line numbers, e.g., 'link.sh, line 45', 'zsh/.zshrc line 181'
Grouped into 'Critical Issues' and 'Structural Improvements' with a summary priority table (High/Medium/Low)

expectations.text: At least 2 findings include concrete suggestions
expectations.evidence: Every finding has a 'Recommendation' section with code examples, e.g., explicit stow package list, PATH consolidation block
